Persian Gulf Oil Tanker Traffic May Never Fully Recover
headlines.summary: Oil and gas traffic via the Strait of Hormuz may never go back to pre-war levels if Iran cements its hold over the chokepoint. The warnings come from several unrelated sources as the war continues to drag on, with some recovery in traffic but nowhere near pre-February 28 levels.

headlines.title: Guyana Emerges as an Oil Winner in the Iran War
headlines.summary: In just seven years, Guyana has become one of the fastest-growing oil exporters and global economies and an oil-producing powerhouse with nearly 1 million barrels per day of production capacity. And the war in Iran is only turning up the heat even more. Since ExxonMobil launched

headlines.title: Rystad: U.S.-Iran Re-Escalation Could Drive Oil To $180 By August
headlines.summary: An acute re-escalation in the U.S.-Iran conflict and a prolonged blockage of the Strait of Hormuz could drive global crude oil prices to $180 per barrel by August, Jorge León, Head of Geopolitical Analysis at Rystad Energy, has projected on CNBC's Squawk Box Europe. According to

headlines.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Refinery Deep Behind Front Lines
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones targeted a Russian oil refinery hundreds of kilometers from the Ukrainian border, the latest in a campaign of deep strikes aimed at disrupting Russia’s oil industry. headlines.title: Pakistan Inflation Accelerates to 11.7% on Oil and Gas Import Shock
headlines.summary: The surge in international oil and gas prices amid the Iran war hiked Pakistan's inflation to the highest level in two years as energy import costs ballooned. headlines.title: Inside The Iran Deal That May Change Nothing — But Could Smash Oil Prices Anyway
headlines.summary: After more than three months of fighting and stop-start negotiations, Washington and Tehran are now reportedly on the verge of a deal that would end the conflict.
